root_text.an8.46:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an8.46:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā anuruddho divāvihāraṁ gato hoti paṭisallīno.
root_text.an8.46:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā manāpakāyikā devatā yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā devatā āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an8.46:1.4: “mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, manāpakāyikā nāma devatā tīsu ṭhānesu issariyaṁ kārema vasaṁ vattema.
root_text.an8.46:1.5: Mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, yādisakaṁ vaṇṇaṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ vaṇṇaṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma;
root_text.an8.46:1.6: yādisakaṁ saraṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ saraṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma;
root_text.an8.46:1.7: yādisakaṁ sukhaṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ sukhaṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma.
root_text.an8.46:1.8: Mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, manāpakāyikā nāma devatā imesu tīsu ṭhānesu issariyaṁ kārema vasaṁ vattemā”ti.
root_text.an8.46:2.2: “aho vatimā devatā sabbāva nīlā assu nīlavaṇṇā nīlavatthā nīlālaṅkārā”ti.
root_text.an8.46:2.3: Atha kho tā devatā āyasmato anuruddhassa cittamaññāya sabbāva nīlā ahesuṁ nīlavaṇṇā nīlavatthā nīlālaṅkārā.
root_text.an8.46:3.4: sabbāva odātā assu odātavaṇṇā odātavatthā odātālaṅkārā”ti.
root_text.an8.46:3.5: Atha kho tā devatā āyasmato anuruddhassa cittamaññāya sabbāva odātā ahesuṁ odātavaṇṇā odātavatthā odātālaṅkārā.
root_text.an8.46:4.1: Atha kho tā devatā ekā ca gāyi ekā ca nacci ekā ca accharaṁ vādesi.
root_text.an8.46:4.2: Seyyathāpi nāma pañcaṅgikassa tūriyassa suvinītassa suppaṭipatāḷitassa kusalehi susamannāhatassa saddo hoti vaggu ca rajanīyo ca kamanīyo ca pemanīyo ca madanīyo ca;
root_text.an8.46:4.3: evamevaṁ tāsaṁ devatānaṁ alaṅkārānaṁ saddo hoti vaggu ca rajanīyo ca kamanīyo ca pemanīyo ca madanīyo ca.
root_text.an8.46:4.4: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho indriyāni okkhipi.
root_text.an8.46:5.1: Atha kho tā devatā “na khvayyo anuruddho sādiyatī”ti tatthevantaradhāyiṁsu.
root_text.an8.46:5.2: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā anuruddho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.46:6.2: Atha kho, bhante, sambahulā manāpakāyikā devatā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho, bhante, tā devatā maṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an8.46:6.3: ‘mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, manāpakāyikā nāma devatā tīsu ṭhānesu issariyaṁ kārema vasaṁ vattema.
root_text.an8.46:6.4: Mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, yādisakaṁ vaṇṇaṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ vaṇṇaṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma;
root_text.an8.46:6.5: yādisakaṁ saraṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ saraṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma;
root_text.an8.46:6.6: yādisakaṁ sukhaṁ ākaṅkhāma tādisakaṁ sukhaṁ ṭhānaso paṭilabhāma.
root_text.an8.46:6.7: Mayaṁ, bhante anuruddha, manāpakāyikā nāma devatā imesu tīsu ṭhānesu issariyaṁ kārema vasaṁ vattemā’ti.
root_text.an8.46:6.9: ‘aho vatimā devatā sabbāva nīlā assu nīlavaṇṇā nīlavatthā nīlālaṅkārā’ti.
root_text.an8.46:6.10: Atha kho, bhante, tā devatā mama cittamaññāya sabbāva nīlā ahesuṁ nīlavaṇṇā nīlavatthā nīlālaṅkārā.
root_text.an8.46:7.4: sabbāva odātā assu odātavaṇṇā odātavatthā odātālaṅkārā’ti.
root_text.an8.46:7.5: Atha kho, bhante, tā devatā mama cittamaññāya sabbāva odātā ahesuṁ odātavaṇṇā odātavatthā odātālaṅkārā.
root_text.an8.46:8.1: Atha kho, bhante, tā devatā ekā ca gāyi ekā ca nacci ekā ca accharaṁ vādesi.
root_text.an8.46:8.2: Seyyathāpi nāma pañcaṅgikassa tūriyassa suvinītassa suppaṭipatāḷitassa kusalehi susamannāhatassa saddo hoti vaggu ca rajanīyo ca kamanīyo ca pemanīyo ca madanīyo ca;
root_text.an8.46:8.3: evamevaṁ tāsaṁ devatānaṁ alaṅkārānaṁ saddo hoti vaggu ca rajanīyo ca kamanīyo ca pemanīyo ca madanīyo ca.
root_text.an8.46:9.1: Atha kho, bhante, tā devatā ‘na khvayyo anuruddho sādiyatī’ti tatthevantaradhāyiṁsu.
root_text.an8.46:9.2: Katihi nu kho, bhante, dhammehi samannāgato mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā manāpakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti?
root_text.an8.46:10.1: “Aṭṭhahi kho, anuruddha, dhammehi samannāgato mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā manāpakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an8.46:10.3: Idha, anuruddha, mātugāmo yassa mātāpitaro bhattuno denti atthakāmā hitesino anukampakā anukampaṁ upādāya tassa hoti pubbuṭṭhāyinī pacchānipātinī kiṅkārapaṭissāvinī manāpacārinī piyavādinī.
root_text.an8.46:11.2: mātāti vā pitāti vā samaṇabrāhmaṇāti vā—
root_text.an8.46:11.3: te sakkaroti, garuṁ karoti, māneti, pūjeti, abbhāgate ca āsanodakena paṭipūjeti.
root_text.an8.46:12.1: Ye te bhattu abbhantarā kammantā—uṇṇāti vā kappāsāti vā—tattha dakkhā hoti analasā tatrupāyāya vīmaṁsāya samannāgatā alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ.
root_text.an8.46:13.1: Yo so bhattu abbhantaro antojano—dāsāti vā pessāti vā kammakarāti vā—
root_text.an8.46:13.2: tesaṁ katañca katato jānāti akatañca akatato jānāti, gilānakānañca balābalaṁ jānāti khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyañcassa paccaṁsena saṁvibhajati.
root_text.an8.46:14.1: Yaṁ bhattu āharati dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā taṁ ārakkhena guttiyā sampādeti, tattha ca hoti adhuttī athenī asoṇḍī avināsikā.
root_text.an8.46:15.1: Upāsikā kho pana hoti buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā.
root_text.an8.46:16.2: pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā, adinnādānā paṭiviratā, kāmesumicchācārā paṭiviratā, musāvādā paṭiviratā, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭiviratā.
root_text.an8.46:17.2: Vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgā payatapāṇinī vossaggaratā yācayogā dānasaṁvibhāgaratā.
root_text.an8.46:18.1: Imehi kho, anuruddha, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā manāpakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatīti.

translation_text.an8.46:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an8.46:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Anuruddha had gone into retreat for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.an8.46:1.3: Then several deities of the Agreeable Host went up to Venerable Anuruddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an8.46:1.4: “Honorable Anuruddha, we are the deities called ‘Agreeable’. We wield authority and control over three things.
translation_text.an8.46:1.5: We can turn any color we want on the spot.
translation_text.an8.46:1.6: We can get any voice that we want on the spot.
translation_text.an8.46:1.7: We can get any pleasure that we want on the spot.
translation_text.an8.46:1.8: We are the deities called ‘Agreeable’. We wield authority and control over these three things.”
translation_text.an8.46:2.2: “If only these deities would all turn blue, of blue color, clad in blue, adorned with blue!”
translation_text.an8.46:2.3: Then those deities, knowing Anuruddha’s thought, all turned blue.
translation_text.an8.46:3.2: “If only these deities would all turn yellow …”
translation_text.an8.46:3.3: “If only these gods would all turn red …”
translation_text.an8.46:3.4: “If only these gods would all turn white …”
translation_text.an8.46:3.5: Then those deities, knowing Anuruddha’s thought, all turned white.
translation_text.an8.46:4.1: Then one of those deities sang, one danced, and one snapped her fingers.
translation_text.an8.46:4.2: Suppose there was a quintet made up of skilled musicians who had practiced well and kept excellent rhythm. They’d sound graceful, tantalizing, sensuous, lovely, and intoxicating.
translation_text.an8.46:4.3: In the same way the performance by those deities sounded graceful, tantalizing, sensuous, lovely, and intoxicating.
translation_text.an8.46:4.4: But Venerable Anuruddha averted his senses.
translation_text.an8.46:5.1: Then those deities, thinking “Master Anuruddha isn’t enjoying this,” vanished right there.
translation_text.an8.46:5.2: Then in the late afternoon, Anuruddha came out of retreat and went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened, adding:
translation_text.an8.46:9.2: “How many qualities do ladies have so that—when their body breaks up, after death—they are reborn in company with the Gods of the Agreeable Host?”
translation_text.an8.46:10.1: “Anuruddha, when they have eight qualities ladies—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in company with the Gods of the Agreeable Host.
translation_text.an8.46:10.3: Take the case of a lady whose mother and father give her to a husband wanting what’s best for her, out of kindness and sympathy. She would get up before him and go to bed after him, and be obliging, behaving nicely and speaking politely.
translation_text.an8.46:11.1: She honors, respects, esteems, and venerates those her husband respects, such as
translation_text.an8.46:11.2: mother and father, and ascetics and brahmins.
translation_text.an8.46:11.3: And when they arrive she serves them with a seat and water.
translation_text.an8.46:12.1: She’s deft and tireless in her husband’s household duties, such as knitting and sewing. She understands how to go about things in order to complete and organize the work.
translation_text.an8.46:13.1: She knows what work her husband’s domestic bondservants, servants, and workers have completed, and what they’ve left incomplete.
translation_text.an8.46:13.2: She knows who is sick, and who is fit or unwell. She distributes to each a fair portion of fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an8.46:14.1: She ensures that any income her husband earns is guarded and protected, whether money, grain, silver, or gold. She doesn’t overspend, steal, waste, or lose it.
translation_text.an8.46:15.1: She’s a lay follower who has gone for refuge to the Buddha, his teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an8.46:16.2: She doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an8.46:17.2: She lives at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.an8.46:18.1: When they possess these eight qualities ladies—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in company with the Gods of the Agreeable Host.
translation_text.an8.46:19.1: She’d never look down on her husband,
translation_text.an8.46:19.2: who’s always eager to work hard,
translation_text.an8.46:20.1: And a good woman never scolds her husband
translation_text.an8.46:21.1: She gets up early, works tirelessly,
translation_text.an8.46:22.1: A lady who fulfills these duties

variant_text.an8.46:4.1: ca → ekāva (sya-all, pts1ed) | ca → ekāva (sya-all, pts1ed) | nacci ekā ca → ekāva (sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an8.46:5.1: na khvayyo → na kho ayyo (bj) | sādiyatī”ti → sādīyatīti (bj); sādayatīti (saddanīti dhātumālā)
variant_text.an8.46:17.2: muttacāgā → muttacāgī (sya-all) | payatapāṇinī → payatapāṇi (bj); payatapāṇī (sya-all, pts1ed, mr)

reference_text.an8.46:1.1: csp1ed17.91, csp2ed17.91, dr23.285, ms17A8_443, msdiv46, pts-vp-pli4.263
